message:
docs(readme): accurate HTML-export self-contained claim [bwscd]

Reality-check bead q6xf9/bwscd surfaced that README.md:307 claimed
"All CSS and JS inlined for offline viewing — no external dependencies
required" while README.md:308 simultaneously claimed "CDN resources
(Tailwind, Prism.js) enhance styling when online, graceful fallback
when offline". The two bullets contradicted each other.

Verification (`cass export-html` on any session):
  - 7 external `<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/...">` tags
    (Tailwind runtime + Prism core + 5 language components)
  - 2 external `<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/...">`
  - 2 inline `<style>` blocks (structural CSS only)
  - 0 inline `<script>` bodies

Prism.js tags carry `onerror="...no-prism"` → code blocks degrade to
monospace when the CDN is unavailable. Tailwind CDN has NO fallback
— utility classes (`flex`, `p-4`, etc.) are generated at runtime from
the Tailwind browser package, so when the CDN fails the page loses
its layout styling entirely.

This commit rewrites the two bullets to match reality:
  - "Mostly Self-Contained" + explicit callout that Tailwind + Prism
    come from jsdelivr
  - "Progressive Enhancement / Graceful Degradation" describes Prism's
    fallback AND flags that Tailwind does NOT currently have one,
    with a note for air-gapped archival users

No code changes — this is a documentation-accuracy fix only. Future
work to actually inline Tailwind + Prism would let us restore the
original claim; until then, the doc matches shipped behavior.

Refs coding_agent_session_search-bwscd

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -302,10 +302,10 @@ Powered by [FrankenTUI (ftui)](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankentui)
 
 ### 📄 HTML Session Export
 
-Export conversations as beautiful, self-contained HTML files with optional encryption:
+Export conversations as styled, portable HTML files with optional encryption:
 
-- **Self-Contained**: All CSS and JS inlined for offline viewing—no external dependencies required
-- **Progressive Enhancement**: CDN resources (Tailwind, Prism.js) enhance styling when online, graceful fallback when offline
+- **Mostly Self-Contained**: Critical structural CSS and the export payload are inlined directly; the file opens without a local web server. Tailwind's utility CSS runtime (`@tailwindcss/browser`) and Prism.js syntax-highlighting assets are loaded from `cdn.jsdelivr.net` for full fidelity.
+- **Progressive Enhancement / Graceful Degradation**: Prism.js resources fall back via `onerror="...no-prism"` — code blocks remain readable offline in plain monospace. Tailwind CDN does not currently have a built-in fallback: layout utilities require network on first open (the page is still legible but unstyled). Air-gapped archival users should note this limitation.
 - **Password Protection**: AES-256-GCM encryption with PBKDF2 key derivation (600,000 iterations)—opens directly in any browser
 - **Rich Styling**: Dark/light themes, syntax-highlighted code blocks, collapsible tool calls
 - **Print-Friendly**: Optimized print styles with page breaks and footers
